title: Cannok

{{Quote|That's the problem with cannoks… if you turn your back on the pests, they'll eat anything they can get in their mouth.|[[Zuka]]|<ref name="TSL" />}}
'''Cannoks''' were small, aggressive predators native to [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]<ref name="Tales of the Jedi 1" /> and its [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]].<ref name="TSL" /> They were relatively harmless to [[Human/Legends|Humans]], but they had a habit of eating anything that could fit in their mouths. They were known as pests, due to a high reproduction rate and notable curiosity. Cannoks were hardy, able to survive the rigors of Dxun by traveling in large groups. 

Several cannoks were transported to [[Telos IV]] by the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]] of [[Citadel Station]] during the years following the [[Jedi Civil War]] to assist in the [[Telosian Restoration Project]], restoring the world to its natural state after the damage done by [[Darth Malak]].<ref name="TSL" />

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|The vicious, wide-jawed predators the Ithorians imported from Dxun.|[[Bao-Dur]]|<ref name="TSL" />}}
[[File:Cannok.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A cannok]]
Cannoks were squat, bloated creatures with normally dull [[Color/Legends|yellow-green]] hides that were characteristically bumpy and wrinkled. The creatures stood on four, broad, tridactyl feet, which ended in tough spatulate claws. They had short, stubby tails and a thick, bony ridge traveling down their back, from which thin, fleshy spines protruded. Their heads had an upward-facing jaw, connected low on the skull, and thin, sharp, needle-like [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] jutting from the lower jaw which protuded over their upper lip. Their [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] stood on short stalks and moved independently of one another.<ref name="TSL" />

==Behavior==
Cannoks were ornery creatures who commonly formed packs or groups to hunt together; these packs were usually formed of three or more cannoks, though single antagonists were not uncommon. The creatures were not easily frightened, and were unafraid of [[Fire/Legends|fire]]. They would not disturb large parties or settlements, though smaller groups were frequently attacked and it was not unheard of for able-bodied men to be [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the beasts. When on the offensive, they usually bit their prey with their large, sharply-lined mouths or head-butted their victims.<ref name="TSL" />

When undisturbed, cannoks tended to react along a set pattern. While walking in their slightly high-stepped gait, they commonly stopped and looked around by moving their eyestalks in a wiggling motion. Cannoks emitted only a husky kind of grunt.<ref name="TSL" />

==History==
[[File:CannokTOTJ.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A cannok on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] in [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]]]]
Cannoks, in existence since before [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]],<ref name="Tales of the Jedi 1">[[Tales of the Jedi 1|''Tales of the Jedi'' 1]]</ref> were originally found on [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]]—the [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]—where they were one of the high rungs on the food chain, beneath both the [[maalraas]], [[boma]] and the [[Zakkeg/Legends|zakkeg]]. Particularly tough cannoks could often scare away maalraas, but the zakkeg easily dined on the abundant creatures.<ref name="TSL" />

They survived the [[Mandalorian Wars]], and afterward, caused headaches for the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Mandalorian Outpost (Dxun)|outpost]] located on the moon. The [[Mandalorians (Clan Ordo)|Mandalorians]] killed all they came across, though the numbers of the quickly-reproducing [[Species/Legends|species]] never seemed to diminish. This group thought of the creatures as "vermin" and unworthy of being killed by such skilled soldiers; being assigned to clear out a herd of the beasts was seen as being the equivalent of punishment duty due to their minor difficulty.<ref name="TSL" />

The omnivorous creatures also caused trouble by frequently eating anything and everything they could fit into their large mouths when no one was looking. This tendency was the most frustrating aspect of their behavior to most [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]]—such as when three of them devoured components of a phase-pulse converter under construction by [[Zuka]], a [[tech specialist]]. When [[Meetra Surik]] arrived on the jungle moon in [[3951 BBY]], she slew and gutted many cannoks' oversized stomachs in the vicinity of the encampment and recovered the converter's components for the Mandalorians in order to maintain their base of operations on Dxun, earning favor with their [[Mandalore (title)|Mandalore]], also known as [[Canderous Ordo]].<ref name="TSL" />
[[File:FrolickingPuppy-KOTOR2.png|thumb|284x284px|A Cannok in a restoration zone of Telos IV]]
During the [[Telosian Restoration Project|restoration]] of [[Telos IV]], several cannoks were brought to the surface of Telos to balance the ecosystem, keeping the herbivore numbers in check so they did not destroy all of the remaining [[Plant/Legends|plant]] life. Cannoks were chosen for this task because of their ability to adapt, and because of their connections with Onderon, which was aiding in the restoration effort. However, the plan went awry, as the cannok population grew exponentially. They soon took over the ecosystem, consuming the herbivores and extingushing many native species permanently. This created a food shortage for them, and their still increasing population only worsened the issue, leaving them hungry as well as easily agitated.<ref name="TSL" />

==Behind the scenes==
The cannoks first appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi 1|first issue]] of [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (miniseries)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comics]], but had only a minor role in the storyline. Eleven years later, the cannoks returned in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', appearing on both the surface of Telos and the moon of Dxun, where more information on the beasts was revealed.
